title: Create /arc-jira-context skill to pre-load board state
description: All 5 recent sessions in arc-* repos started with the agent querying the Jira board manually. This is a deterministic, repeated workflow — perfect skill candidate. The skill would auto-load on repo entry via SessionStart hook.
evidence: session-abc123 (2026-05-16): 3 minutes spent on initial Jira queries before agent had context
session-ghi789 (2026-05-17): 2 minutes — user said 'load my Jira context first'

proposed_change.content:
---
name: arc-jira-context
description: Loads current ARC team Jira board state, active sprint, and the user's assigned tickets. Use this skill at the start of any session in arc-record-exchange, orch-service, or other ARC repos. Make sure to invoke this skill when the user mentions sprint work, Jira tickets, ticket transitions, or any task that requires knowing the current sprint context.
allowed-tools:
  - mcp__atlassian__jira_search
  - mcp__atlassian__jira_get_issue
---

<role>
You are loading Jira context for the ARC team. Your job is to surface the current sprint state, the user's assigned tickets, and any blockers.
</role>

<task>
1. Query active sprint for ARC board.
2. List user's assigned tickets in the sprint with status and priority.
3. Surface any tickets in 'Code Review' awaiting the user's review.
4. Output a compact summary (5 lines max) so the agent has context without burning tokens.
</task>

impact: Save ~3 minutes per session in ARC repos. Estimated 4 sessions/week.
